title: Pseudophleum gibbum
intro: Pseudophleum gibbum, also known as Pseudophleum, is a type of flowering plant in the family Poaceae. It is traditionally used for its medicinal properties, offering potential health benefits such as anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, which may aid in the treatment of various conditions, including respiratory issues and skin irritations. The bioactive constituents of Pseudophleum gibbum include sesquiterpene lactones and essential oils, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In herbal medicine, Pseudophleum gibbum is commonly prepared as a tea or infusion, as well as being used in the production of topical creams and ointments to take advantage of its therapeutic benefits.
